title: sorbus parvifolia
intro: Sorbus parvifolia, also known as the dwarf mountain ash or Chinese mountain ash, is a deciduous shrub native to the mountainous regions of Asia. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its bark, which contains salicin, a compound similar to aspirin. The health benefits of Sorbus parvifolia include reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and treating fever, due to its salicin content. Herbal preparations of Sorbus parvifolia are typically made from its bark, and can be found in various forms, including teas, tinctures, and infusions.
